source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As shown in the figure, a small object (which can be considered as a point mass) is placed on a thin cardboard on a table. A constant horizontal force $F$ is applied to the right to pull the cardboard. Depending on the magnitude of the force, the motion of the cardboard and the small object will vary. The mass of the cardboard is $m_{1}=0.1\mathrm{kg}$, the mass of the small object is $m_{2}=0.4\mathrm{kg}$, and the distance between the small object and the right edge of the table is $d=0.15\mathrm{m}$. The coefficient of kinetic friction between all contact surfaces is $\mu=0.2$, and the maximum static friction is equal to the kinetic friction. The acceleration due to gravity is $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. When the small object moves together with the cardboard, the magnitude of the frictional force exerted by the tabletop on the cardboard.
2. Under what condition does the pulling force $F$ satisfy for the small object to experience relative sliding with the cardboard?
3. If the pulling force acts for 0.3 seconds and the cardboard is just pulled out from under the small object, determine whether the small object will remain on the table through calculation.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
$1\mathrm{N}$
$F>2\mathrm{N}$
Small objects will not remain on the tabletop. Do not add any other content.

image_dependency: 题干显式写有“As shown in the figure”，说明出题时依赖示意图呈现物体、纸板、桌面的位置关系。
输入字段中 requires_image=true 且 text_dominant=false，支持该题具有较强图像依赖。
不过题干文字已给出主要物理量与关键距离 d=0.15 m，核心求解信息大部分可由文本恢复，因此图像依赖不评为满分。
multi_step: 题目包含 3 个连续小问：先求摩擦力，再求发生相对滑动的力的条件，最后结合 0.3 s 的作用时间判断物块是否留在桌面上。
需要组合多个条件与阶段：静/动摩擦、系统加速度、相对运动判据、位移比较，明显属于多步推理。
第 3 问不仅要计算，还要把前面的运动结论与桌边距离 d 结合，具备较强过程链条。
verifiability: 答案主要是数值、条件不等式和最终二元判断（1 N、F>2 N、是否留在桌面），结果形式清晰。
给定了 m1、m2、d、μ、g 和作用时间 0.3 s，关键中间量与结论都可用物理公式复核。
asset_registry_record 中 answer_present=true、answer_type=short_text、registry_passed=true，说明样本资产完整，便于后续核验。

risk_flags: 示意图可能主要用于场景理解，但文本已包含大部分定量信息，实际图像必要性可能低于 requires_image 标记。

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和主图像均存在，registry_passed=true，且无显式 issues。
初步评分高：multi_step_score=0.92、verifiability_score=0.94，说明样本具有较强推理价值且便于核验。
图像依赖较强但可解释：image_dependency_score=0.74，且图文结合能支持核心语义恢复，不构成入池障碍。
风险标记仅指出图像必要性可能被高估，并未表明核心语义缺失或样本不可用。

As shown in the figure, a small object (which can be considered as a point mass) is placed on a thin cardboard on a table. A constant horizontal force $F$ is applied to the right to pull the cardboard. Depending on the magnitude of the force, the motion of the cardboard and the small object will vary. The mass of the cardboard is $m_{1}=0.1\mathrm{kg}$, the mass of the small object is $m_{2}=0.4\mathrm{kg}$, and the distance between the small object and the right edge of the table is $d=0.15\mathrm{m}$. The coefficient of kinetic friction between all contact surfaces is $\mu=0.2$, and the maximum static friction is equal to the kinetic friction. The acceleration due to gravity is $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. When the small object moves together with the cardboard, determine the magnitude of the frictional force exerted by the tabletop on the cardboard.
2. Under what condition on the pulling force $F$ will the small object experience relative sliding with the cardboard?
3. If the pulling force acts for 0.3 seconds and the cardboard is just pulled out from under the small object, determine whether the small object will remain on the table through calculation.
normalized_value.expected_answer:
$1\mathrm{N}$
$F>2\mathrm{N}$
Small objects will not remain on the tabletop.

agent_assessment.rationale: The problem statement is fully specified with masses, friction, distance, time, and required sub-questions. The image is a clear schematic that supports the setup (object on cardboard on table, pull direction, and distance to edge). A human annotator can understand both the task and the expected answers.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The sample is complete and jointly understandable, the answer is materially available, the rewrite is usable as a single open variant, and alignment/solvability reports show no serious issues.
